articles.title: Sweden to ban mobile phones in schools as country shifts back to books and traditional learning
articles.description: Sweden will ban mobile phones in schools from the next academic year as part of a broader push to reduce screen time, improve reading skills and promote traditional learning methods.

articles.title: "Party is slipping out of her hands": BJP's Shehzad Poonawalla claims 60 TMC MLAs, distancing themselves from Mamata Banerjee
articles.description: Bharatiya Janata Party leader Shehzad Poonawalla alleges internal discord within the Trinamool Congress. He claims many TMC MLAs and MPs are unhappy with the leadership of Mamata Banerjee and Abhishek Banerjee. This comes after TMC leader Kirti Azad dismissed reports of a separate group of MPs supporting the NDA. Azad called the claims fake and stated Operation Lotus has failed.

articles.title: The Mahabharat War Code: Ancient Secrets Behind Today’s Global Conflicts | ft. Ami Ganatra
articles.description: The Mahabharat is far more than an ancient epic ...it is a timeless guide to understanding power, strategy, leadership, ethics, and conflict. In this fascinating conversation, we explore how the principles and war strategies described in the Mahabharat continue to resonate in today’s geopolitical landscape and global conflicts. Are nations still making the same mistakes that led to the war of Kurukshetra? What can modern leaders learn from the decisions of Krishna, Arjuna, Bhishma, and Duryodhana? Join us as we decode the enduring relevance of the Mahabharat and uncover lessons that remain strikingly relevant in an increasingly complex world. ft. Ami Ganatra#timesmantra #mahabharat #amiganatra #kurukshetra #geopolitics #ancientwisdom #indianepics #worldhistory #leadership #strategythinking

articles.title: Ben Stokes nightclub controversy: What happened between England captain, Gus Atkinson and rugby player?
articles.description: Ben Stokes and Gus Atkinson are under ECB investigation after breaching a midnight curfew and becoming involved in a nightclub altercation in Chelsea. Reports claim a Saracens academy rugby player threw a punch at Atkinson but hit an ECB security guard, who needed stitches. ECB sources insist the cricketers “were not the aggressors.” Stokes’s captaincy is now under scrutiny.

articles.title: Manipur DGP visits Kangpokpi, Senapati as fresh protests, highway blockade and census opposition intensify
articles.description: Manipur's new DGP Mukesh Singh visited Kangpokpi and Senapati districts to assess the law and order situation. He assured impartial police action and urged peaceful coexistence. Meanwhile, vehicular movement on NH-202 is suspended due to protests against alleged excessive force on women. A rally also demanded the postponement of the census exercise, citing displacement of conflict-affected people.

articles.title: Another war front opens? Trump's Mid-East ally hit by missile scare as it vanishes near Yemen border
articles.description: Panic spread across the Gulf after air raid sirens sounded in Saudi Arabia, sparking speculation that a key Saudi military base had come under attack. Riyadh later denied the reports, saying a missile launched from Yemen had deviated from its course and triggered precautionary alerts. The incident unfolded as Iran and Israel exchanged fresh strikes, while Yemen’s Houthis claimed new missile attacks on Israeli targets and threatened shipping routes in the Red Sea. With Donald Trump urging both sides to stop firing, fears of a wider Middle East war are once again rising.

articles.title: Spanish proverb of the day: 'An old man who marries a young wife buys...' Life lessons on marriage, death, relationships, human nature, and why one should marry for the right reasons
articles.description: Spanish proverb of the day highlights a traditional saying that warns about large age gaps in marriage and the challenges they may create. The proverb discusses relationships, marriage, life stages, human nature, stress, expectations, and long-term compatibility. It also explores lessons about choosing a partner for the right reasons and explains why this traditional Spanish proverb continues to remain relevant in modern society.

articles.title: Ukraine’s fatal miscalculation: Zelensky believes he can handle what comes after Oreshnik and Kedr?
articles.description: Has Vladimir Putin just rejected what Ukraine claims was Russia’s best chance for a favorable peace deal? A new war of words has erupted between Kyiv and Moscow after Ukrainian Foreign Minister Andrii Sybiha declared that Putin had “missed his chance” to end the war by refusing fresh negotiations and rejecting Volodymyr Zelensky’s proposal for direct face-to-face talks. Kyiv is now arguing that time is no longer on Russia’s side and that the military, economic and diplomatic costs of the war will only continue to grow. The remarks came as Zelensky met with British Prime Minister Keir Starmer, French President Emmanuel Macron and German Chancellor Friedrich Merz, with Europe stepping up efforts to shape the next phase of the conflict. The London talks focused on a possible ceasefire and broader peace efforts, while European leaders backed direct Ukraine-Russia dialogue with U.S. involvement. At the same time, tensions are rising over Ukraine’s growing long-range strike ambitions. Zelensky suggested that Kyiv is close to fielding a ballistic missile capable of hitting targets inside Russia, a development that could add a new dimension to the conflict. Some analysts believe Ukraine is attempting to increase pressure on Moscow, while others warn that the move risks provoking a stronger Russian response. Meanwhile, Russia dismissed Zelensky’s public appeal for talks, with senior officials arguing that the Ukrainian leader’s open letter showed Kyiv had “no other option.” Donald Trump also weighed in, saying he “doesn’t mind” if Putin and Zelensky meet in person, adding another layer to the diplomatic debate. Is Russia really losing leverage? Has Europe found a new opening for peace? And what happens if diplomacy fails while both sides continue to escalate? This report examines the latest diplomatic confrontation, the growing pressure campaign against Moscow, Europe’s renewed peace push, and why the next few months could prove critical for the future of the war.

articles.title: ‘Rogue U.S Cities & States Not Cooperating’: Rep. Arrington DESTROYS House Dems In Roaring Testimony
articles.description: A fiery debate over immigration enforcement erupted on Capitol Hill as Republican Representative Jodey Arrington defended increased funding for border security and ICE operations while criticizing Democratic opposition. During a heated hearing, Arrington argued that some cities and states were failing to cooperate with federal immigration authorities, describing the situation as a major obstacle to enforcing U.S. immigration laws. The testimony quickly became one of the most contentious moments of the hearing, drawing strong reactions from both sides. Watch.

The decision crucially protects the vital employment pipeline for highly skilled Indian professionals, who systematically secure the majority of these 85,000 annual US work permits.

articles.title: Israel and Iran step back from further strikes after renewed clashes
articles.description: Israel and Iran appeared to pull back from further military action on Monday after exchanging fire for the first time since a US-brokered ceasefire two months ago. Both sides warned they would retaliate if attacked again, while regional powers and the Trump administration pushed to prevent the conflict from escalating into a wider war.

articles.title: 'Just Because Of Dems' Reckless Extremism...': Rep. Foxx Tears Apart Rep. Scanlon In ICE House Clash
articles.description: A heated House hearing on a proposed $70 billion funding boost for U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) erupted into a fierce partisan clash as Republican Representative Virginia Foxx took aim at Democratic lawmaker Mary Gay Scanlon. Foxx accused Democrats of promoting what she called “reckless extremism” on immigration, arguing that stronger enforcement and expanded ICE resources are essential for border security and public safety. Scanlon pushed back, raising concerns over oversight, civil liberties and the agency’s growing powers. Watch.

articles.title: 'Did Your Mom Use A Cond*m?': Epstein Accusers Get Guns After Trump DOJ's Shock Action | WATCH
articles.description: For nearly two dozen women who accused Jeffrey Epstein of abuse, speaking out came at a heavy personal cost. In interviews with Reuters, the accusers described years of threats, harassment, and intimidation that intensified after Justice Department files exposed their identities. Many say they became targets of relentless online attacks and abuse, forcing them to alter their daily lives. Some now live in constant fear, taking extraordinary measures to protect themselves, including carrying weapons. Their accounts reveal the enduring fallout faced by survivors long after the headlines fade and the court cases end. Watch.

articles.title: Life lesson of the day by John D. Rockefeller: “Don’t be afraid to give up the good to go for the great.” — Why do some people achieve more than others despite similar opportunities? Learn from the man who built one of history’s greatest business empires through vision, discipline, and relentless growth
articles.description: Life Lesson of the Day by John D. Rockefeller offers a powerful success mindset for modern life. Rockefeller built one of historys largest business fortunes by making bold decisions when others chose comfort. His famous quote reveals a timeless truth. Personal growth, leadership success, wealth creation, and life-changing opportunities often begin when people leave good situations for greater possibilities. The lesson is simple yet profound. Growth rarely comes from safety. Greatness often starts with one courageous choice.

articles.title: UP police constable recruitment exam 2025 conducted peacefully across 75 districts; Over 7.23 lakh candidates appear
articles.description: Uttar Pradesh police constable recruitment exams concluded smoothly statewide on Monday, with over 7.23 lakh candidates appearing. Elaborate security and monitoring were in place to prevent malpractice. Authorities registered cases against individuals spreading false information and attempting unfair means, including two arrests for possessing mobile phones during the exam. Strict action is promised against any compromise to the recruitment process.
